text.heading: Calling Igbinedion University
text.p: SIR: I wish to use this medium to appeal to the Igbinedion University authorities to act fast and save the lives of its students from the present epidemic ravaging the students due to the poor quality of water they drink.

The students are either sick or recovering from one ailment or the other at present and if this case is not addressed immediately, the school authorities might find themselves in a big dilemma that will overwhelm them. The supply of clean drinking water in the university has become a mirage hence students who cannot afford the expensive but scarce bottled water end up drinking this bad water.

The situation is made worse now due to the hot weather and the only means of quenching their (students) thirst is to drink the only available water within their premises. Cases of water-borne ailments have risen within the past couple of months and most of these students have had to visit health clinics frequently due to the health complications now paramount in the school. Many have been taken home for proper medical attention only to return back to the same precarious situation.

The school authorities need to act now as we are talking about human lives here and a stitch in time, they say, saves nine. Appropriate soil tests need to be carried out and then boreholes with treatment plants should be constructed immediately in strategic locations within the university.

Parents are making adequate financial sacrifices in Okada hence their children deserve the basic school amenities which include supply of potable drinking water. Budding young ones should be protected from such ‘poisonous’ situation.
Also the issue of the School Clinic not opening on Sundays needs to be addressed as emergencies give no prior notice and can occur at any given time or day.
